name: Wowza Streaming Engine / Wowza Video API
what_it_does: Wowza REST API for live video streaming infrastructure. Enables AI agents to manage live streams, configure transcoding profiles, control Wowza Streaming Engine instances, manage VOD content, and monitor stream health. Wowza supports RTMP, HLS, DASH, and WebRTC protocols for enterprise live streaming at scale.
use_cases: Starting and stopping live streams from broadcast automation agents
Configuring transcoding profiles from media operations agents
Monitoring stream health and viewer counts from broadcast management agents
Managing VOD libraries from content operations agents
not_for: Simple video hosting without live streaming needs
Consumer social video without enterprise streaming requirements
best_when: An enterprise broadcaster or media company wants AI agents to manage live streaming infrastructure, automate stream configuration, and monitor broadcast operations at scale.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Live stream content may include personal data. Wowza Streaming Engine (self-hosted) has different API from Wowza Video (cloud) — use correct product.

auth.notes: Wowza Video uses API key + access key authentication in request headers. Wowza Streaming Engine (self-hosted) uses digest authentication. Keys are account-scoped.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Wowza Video: trial with limited stream hours; Streaming Engine: 30-day free trial
pricing.paid_tiers: Wowza Video: per-stream-hour and bandwidth pricing; Streaming Engine: monthly/annual subscription
pricing.notes: Separate products with different pricing models. Enterprise pricing for large scale.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per account; stream limits per plan tier

known_agent_gotchas: Two separate products: Wowza Video (cloud) vs Wowza Streaming Engine (self-hosted) — different APIs
No public MCP server — REST API with key-based auth
Stream lifecycle management requires careful state tracking (idle, starting, started, stopping)
Transcoding profiles must be configured before stream start
RTMP ingest URLs are generated per-stream and expire
PRIVACY: Live broadcast content may include personal or sensitive video
agent_readiness.security_notes: API key + access key auth. SOC2. Separate cloud vs self-hosted security postures. Live content privacy considerations.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. SOC2. Live broadcast content privacy. Self-hosted Streaming Engine has variable security configuration.
